The use of corticosteroids in acute asthma exacerbations is indeed justified because their anti-inflammatory properties effectively manage the underlying cause—chronic airway inflammation—which drives many symptoms seen in these episodes. However, while corticosteroids are highly effective, they must also be used judiciously alongside short-acting beta agonists (SABAs) for immediate relief since SABA alone may only address spasm without reducing inflammation.
